rationale: The page contains significant technical information on medical advancements including oncolytic vaccines, personalized mRNA vaccines, and self-amplifying mRNA (saRNA) technology. It also covers international health policy regarding the WHO Pandemic Agreement, making it highly suitable for generating multiple-choice questions.
cleanedText:
Recombinant Chimeric Vaccine: A chimeric vaccine refers to vaccine that combines antigens from different stages of a parasite's life cycle into a single immunogen.
About Malaria
• Life-threatening disease caused by Plasmodium parasites.
• Malaria mostly spreads to people through bites of some infected female Anopheles mosquitoes.
• Five Plasmodium species cause malaria in humans: P. falciparum, P. vivax, P. malariae, P. ovale, P. knowlesi.
5.5.2. ONCOLYTIC & PERSONALIZED MRNA VACCINES
Why in the News?
Russia is developing oncolytic vaccine Enteromix and Personalized messenger RNA (mRNA) cancer vaccines for cancer treatment.
About Oncolytic Vaccine Enteromix
• An Oncolytic vaccine uses oncolytic viruses (OVs) to directly kill cancer cells and stimulate an anti-tumor immune response.
• Enteromix is based on a combination of four non-pathogenic viruses that have the ability to destroy malignant cells and simultaneously activate the patient's antitumor immunity.
About Personalized mRNA Vaccines
• Personalized Vaccine: Based on the genetic analysis of each patient's cancer cells.
• mRNA vaccines: The vaccine contains a synthetic mRNA strand that encodes a specific protein from the virus.
• mRNA delivers genetic material, encapsulated in lipid nanoparticles, into body to prompt protein production that match up with parts of pathogen called antigens.
• E.g., the mRNA vaccine for COVID-19 directed cells to produce copies of a protein on the outside of the coronavirus known as the spike protein.
Benefits of mRNA Vaccines
• Rapid Development: mRNA vaccines can be designed quickly once the virus's genetic sequence is known.
• Strong Immune Response: They stimulate both antibody and T-cell immunity for comprehensive protection.
• No Live Virus Needed: This reduces the risk of causing disease while maintaining effectiveness.
Related News: Self-amplifying mRNA (saRNA) Vaccine
• Gennova Biopharmaceuticals is developing a pioneering self-amplifying mRNA (saRNA) vaccine candidate against the Nipah virus, as part of expanded funding from the Coalition for Epidemic Preparedness Innovations (CEPI).
• saRNA vaccines work by giving the body instructions to replicate mRNA needed to create the desired antigen, potentially enhancing the immune response with lower doses compared to existing mRNA vaccines.
5.6. OTHER IMPORTANT NEWS/DEVELOPMENTS
5.6.1. WHO PANDEMIC AGREEMENT
WHO member states held their first meeting of the Intergovernmental Working Group (IGWG) on the WHO Pandemic Agreement.
About WHO Pandemic Agreement
• Second international legal agreement negotiated under Article 19 of the WHO Constitution (first being the WHO Framework Convention on Tobacco Control, 2003).
